text:
Once upon a time, there lived a cheerful hummingbird named Henry. He fluttered through the lavender fields with astonishing speed, visiting flowers of every hue.

One sunny morning, as Henry flew high into the azure sky, he spotted a lone flower suspended far above its usual bed of honeyberries. It glowed with an inner light, like a starbeauty. With determination, Henry fluffed his feathers and pumped his little wings.

Using his quick wit, he realized he couldn't hoist it down with his beak. But he had an idea! He flew to the edge of a cliff and started chirping a tune only he knew. Suddenly, a tiny figure appeared: a baby squirrel.

"Hello, tiny friend!" Henry chirped cheerfully. The squirrel scampered toward him. Henry couldn't believe it—he wasn’t a bird, but a curious little critter!

Using his keen eyesight, Henry demonstrated how he flew up high. The baby squirrel, wide-eyed and curious
